title: brain git rollback <runId> [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: Privileged rollback of a previously-integrated run. The broker challenge carries the deterministic revert-commit derivation, so the authorized revert is exactly the one presented. Operation-class semantics: a capture-only run tombstones/deactivates its rendition + capture (the raw blob is retained); a run with downstream dependents is refused with the stable code "has-dependents" (exit 1), listing the dependent runs and pointing at the compensating-ChangePlan path; a self-contained run is reverted directly. Rollback is a DISTINCT, operator-initiated run (recovery-state-machine.md `rolled-back` terminal): it does NOT re-open or mutate the reverted run, which stays `finalized`. The reverting run has its own identity (`rollbackRunId`) and links to the reverted run via `rollbackOf`. A mandatory reconciliation runs after the revert, and exactly one run.rolled_back ledger event is emitted. Authorized ONLY by an OS-presence assertion bound to the broker challenge (op git rollback) or the non-interactive --export-challenge → sign → --authorization flow (security-broker-contract.md §7); --yes never authorizes. Without an authorization, exits 6 (action-required) and, with --export-challenge, emits an AuthorizationChallenge (security-broker-contract.md §7.1) instead of this success object.

runId.description: ULID run id being rolled back (the reverted/original run; stays finalized).

rollbackRunId.description: ULID id of the DISTINCT operator-initiated rollback run whose terminal is rolled-back (recovery-state-machine.md). Not equal to runId.

rollbackOf.description: The reverted run's id this rollback run links to (equals runId); mirrors the recovery state machine's rollbackOf.

revertCommit.description: 40-char hex deterministic revert commit integrated onto canonical.

class.description: The operation class that determined the rollback path: capture-only ⇒ tombstone/deactivate rendition+capture; self-contained ⇒ direct revert; compensating ⇒ a compensating-ChangePlan was applied.

reconciled.description: Mandatory completion postcondition: an exit-0 rollback success ALWAYS reflects a completed post-revert reconciliation. An unreconciled / in-progress outcome is not this success object.

dependents.description: Downstream dependent run ids (empty on success; populated only in the has-dependents refusal error surface).

x-atlas-contract.summary: Broker-authorized rollback of an integrated run as a distinct run (rollbackOf-linked): operation-class semantics (capture-only tombstone / self-contained revert / has-dependents refusal), mandatory reconciliation, single run.rolled_back.

args.description: The integrated run to roll back.

flags.description: Emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge (op git rollback, carrying the deterministic revert-commit derivation) to stdout and exit 6 instead of rolling back; sign it out of band with an enrolled approver key.
Path to a signed AuthorizationResponse produced from a prior --export-challenge; presents it to the broker to authorize the rollback.

Caller-supplied idempotency key; a repeat rollback with the same key + request-hash (requestHashScope) returns the existing rollback run rather than reverting twice (registry idempotency: key-accepting).

sideEffects: capture-only: tombstone/deactivate rendition + capture (raw blob retained)
self-contained: integrate the deterministic revert commit onto canonical
prohibitedEffects: never rolls back a run with downstream dependents directly (refuses with has-dependents; requires a compensating ChangePlan)
never discards a retained raw blob on a capture-only rollback
never authorized by --yes; never bypasses the broker challenge
never emits more than one run.rolled_back event per run
never runs without the canonical-integration exclusive lock

errorCodes.when: unknown flag/argument, missing <runId>, or --export-challenge combined with --authorization
no --authorization supplied (challenge exported with --export-challenge)
broker drift: the derived revertCommit differs from the challenge (security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker drift: canonicalBaseCommit is no longer the canonical tip
broker: the challenge nonce expired (expiresAt passed)
broker: the OS presence assertion is absent or not bound to the challenge
broker: Ed25519 signature verification failed
broker: signingPayload differs from the broker-recomputed canonical bytes
broker: the challenge/response fails the contracts schema (universal broker validation failure, security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: the response's payloadCanonicalization id is unknown (universal broker validation failure, security-broker-contract.md §7.3)
broker: signerId is not in the signer registry
broker: the signer is revoked
broker: the signer is not permitted for this op
broker: the nonce was not issued by this broker
broker: the nonce was already consumed
the run has downstream dependents; a compensating ChangePlan is required (stable code, lists dependents)
<runId> does not exist or is not an integrated, rollback-eligible run
another integration holder is active
the fail-closed backup watermark blocks a ledger-writing run
unexpected internal failure during rollback
